changes.before: Here it made her swallow against air.
She brushed the mop bucket with her calf, caught the shelf edge with her sleeve, and kept her eyes on the latch and the nearest torch bracket.
changes.after: They moved deeper into the passage network, past service stairs, linen shelves, and a bend clogged by a mop bucket and a broken crate.

changes.before: Sylvanas's gaze dropped to the blood on Jaina's hand.

Prince-shaped. Blond hair gone dull. Broad shoulders in plate dark around the joints with frost that did not melt. The line of the jaw she had once touched in rooms with no witnesses and no kingdoms in them.
changes.after: Prince-shaped. Blond hair gone dull. Broad shoulders in plate dark around the joints with frost that did not melt.

changes.before: His hand lay palm-up beside the signet ring, fingers still curling closed.
changes.after: Blood spread across the marble, bright where the sunlight caught it.

changes.before: Sylvanas drew him across the chamber in a running diagonal that used every column base, every shift in light, every line in the floor inlay that gave a heel more or less trust. She never retreated straight. Never gave him the center of her back.
changes.after: Jaina's stomach seized as Sylvanas gave him the diagonal, one column base at a time, buying Jaina's opening with her own body.

changes.before: Sylvanas glanced upward. Only a glance. Enough.
Tears came by force before she had room for sorrow. Her skull felt split. Her ears rang with voices that were not voices, memory skinned raw of context.
changes.after: Tears came by force before she had room for sorrow. Her skull felt split. Her ears rang with voices that were not voices, memory skinned raw of context.

changes.before: "Do you have a containment spell left in you?"
changes.after: "Do you have a containment spell left in you?"

changes.before: "Drop your weapons!" ... "look at the room, we killed him."
changes.after: "Drop your weapons!" remains, but surrounding narration was cut so the shouted command lands faster.

changes.before: The sound was small. Final enough anyway.
changes.after: She set it on the marble.

wordcount: Opening hallway beat cut vestigial timing language ('one heartbeat') so the scene enters cleaner and faster.
Bread-taste reaction tightened from abstract choking to direct disgust, keeping Jaina inside the body instead of at narrative remove.
Service-corridor movement lost redundant object choreography; the clutter remains, the prose stops narrating every brush and catch.
Uther encounter dropped explanatory filler around the functionary so the scene reaches the tactical question faster.
Jaina's explanation to Uther stayed technical but was compressed to the essential handoff: turn, sequence, survival risk.
Pre-door threshold lost repeated stage business and came in sharper on the voices through the wall.
Throne-room entrance shed one extra physical memory beat on Arthas so the reveal lands on wrongness, not reminiscence.
Mid-fight section cut repeated staff recovery and knife-exchange detail after the tactical point was already made.
Post-kill aftermath lost one full paragraph of restated predicament; the custody trap now arrives as immediate fact.
Total chapter reduced from 5111 words to 4148, a cut of 963 words while preserving every required plot beat.
hitlist: Eye/gaze vocabulary dropped where it was doing simple notice-work ('gaze dropped' -> 'saw it').
Opening fragments still carry pressure, but several explanatory tail clauses were removed so they stop sounding line-edited.
Stacked throne-room description was thinned to one visual lane at a time instead of piling architecture, light, and memory in the same beat.
A repeated narrator reminder that concealment had ended was cut once the action itself made it obvious.
Repeated references to Arthas's throat knitting were trimmed after the first grotesque proof landed.
Combat geography stayed intact, but duplicated statements about Sylvanas never retreating straight and using every line in the room were compressed into one tactical image.
The black-red surge paragraph lost decorative chewiness while keeping the bread comparison that matters to Jaina's sensory lane.
Multiple short fragments around the second sword crack were reduced so the rhythm sharpens instead of stuttering.
The post-impact Sylvanas injury beat lost an extra explanatory sentence; the throw into the column already carries the damage.
Jaina's retreat over the gold seal kept the tactical problem and lost one repeated statement about needing distance.
The guard-entry tableau compressed a list of visual evidence into one verdict sentence so the prose stops counting exhibits.
Custody sequence lost one explanatory line about treason being heard in the room; the spearpoints and Uther's wording already imply it.
Final corridor exit kept the bread callback and cut redundant recap nouns wherever the chamber image had already lodged.
No document-voice offenders from the anti-slop list survived in narration; the pass focused on compression, repetition, and physical immediacy.
Simile load remained low at one 'like' and one 'as if'; both were kept because each does distinct work under peak pressure.
dialogue: Quoted speech was already largely clean of bureaucratic or clinical contamination; no spoken line read like a memo or report.
Sylvanas's lines stayed clipped and tactical after cuts; none were inflated into editor-smooth reassurance.
Jaina's mage vocabulary stayed inside her voice sheet ceiling: 'sequence,' 'seam,' and 'containment spell' remain character-grounded in crisis.
Fake simplification was not present in this chapter; when characters ask practical questions, the answers cash out into actions, timing, or risk.
Guard, captain, and Uther dialogue all remained scene-native; no line required register downgrading beyond surrounding prose compression.

flags: Kept 'containment spell' in dialogue because this is endgame crisis speech from Jaina, not memo language.
Kept the single 'as if' construction on the soul-scream because it marks impossible force, not decorative comparison fog.
